pi_items.source: GMAT script execution confirms the target Delta-V is ~107.88 m/s, which satisfies this lower bound.
GMAT execution yields a required Delta-V of 0.10788 km/s (107.88 m/s). The verifier strictly enforces a maximum of 15.0, which incorrectly fails a properly converted answer.
GMAT execution confirms the time to crossing is ~94.06 days, satisfying this lower bound.
GMAT execution confirms the time to crossing is ~94.06 days, satisfying this upper bound.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically defined DSCOVR spacecraft state near L1. By executing the required Differential Corrector targeting loop in GMAT, the true required X-axis Delta-V is calculated to be 0.10788 km/s (107.88 m/s), and the time to the X-Z plane crossing is approximately 94.06 days. There is a critical defect in the task verifier: it strictly enforces a maximum delta_v_x_m_s of 15.0, meaning an agent that correctly converts the GMAT output from km/s to m/s will fail the verifier, whereas an agent that erroneously leaves the value unconverted as 0.10788 will falsely pass.
